methods.description: Process for extracting and cleaning otoliths from fish skulls for further morphological and morphometric analysis.

steps.description: Skulls were opened ventrally and right and left otoliths were removed.
Otoliths were cleaned from tissue remains in 1% potassium hydroxide (KOH) solution for 6 h and then rinsed in distilled water for 12 h.
steps.searchable_summary: clean otoliths using KOH solution and rinse with distilled water

methods.description: Examination of otolith morphology using stereomicroscopy and SEM imaging for detailed structural analysis.

steps.description: Otolith morphology was studied with a stereomicroscope.
Morphological comparisons were conducted using SEM images (LEO 1430 VP) of five representative otoliths from each population.
steps.searchable_summary: compare otolith morphology using SEM images

methods.description: Measurement and standardization of otolith dimensions for statistical analysis.

steps.description: Only left otoliths were used. They were positioned on plasticine with the lateral face down, and digital images were captured using a Leica DFC 295 camera and the IMAGIC 1000 software.
steps.searchable_summary: capture digital images of left otoliths using camera and software

steps.description: Eight linear distances and three angles were measured for each of the otoliths.
steps.searchable_summary: measure linear distances and angles of otoliths

steps.description: Linear measurements were standardized as a function of length and height of otolith, respectively, and, together with the angle values, used as otolith variables in the statistical analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize otolith measurements for statistical analysis

methods.description: Use of groupwise registration approach to create a representative image of otolith contours for a given population.

steps.description: The groupwise registration approach was used for a comprehensive illustration of the otolith contour of a given population.
steps.searchable_summary: apply groupwise registration to illustrate otolith contours
